summary: Crisp audience recording captures a powerhouse second set highlighted by Terrapin, Lovelight and an unforgettable China Doll.
review: The tape delivers a vivid snapshot of Alpine’s early‑80s magic – the second set roars with a fiery Terrapin Station out of space, a soaring Lovelight and a transcendent China Doll, while Jerry’s guitar work is repeatedly praised.  Although it’s an audience capture, listeners note a surprisingly clear mix that even brings out Bob’s wah‑rich Dupree solo.  The first set gets mixed remarks, but overall the performance is celebrated as one of the era’s best.

ai_rating.rationale: A large majority of reviews (15+) are enthusiastic, highlighting standout songs and strong playing; recording is described as crisp for an audience tape with only minor flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp AUD that captures guitar wah tones and overall clarity; a few reviewers note it isn’t perfect soundboard quality but still very listenable.

poor_songs: First set (described as short/lame by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set dominates the night – space builds into a thunderous Terrapin, followed by an explosive Lovelight that segues into Not Fade Away and closes with China Doll.  Energy stays high throughout, making the first set feel secondary.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for inspired, crisp guitar tone and confident solos.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for fire in his bass lines, especially during the Lovelight jam and late‑set moments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for powerful rhythm work and a standout Dupree solo with a thick wah sound.
